Família do Norte.description: Brazilian criminal organization founded in 2007 in Manaus, Amazonas, led by Zé Roberto da Compensa, João Pinto Carioca, and Gelson Carnaúba. It was one of the largest criminal groups in Amazonas, involved in drug trafficking, assassinations, robberies, extortion, and prison rebellions. The group engaged in a violent conflict with the PCC from 2015 onward, culminating in the 2016 prison massacres. It became defunct around 2021 after losing territory to the Comando Vermelho.

Native Syndicate.description: The Native Syndicate is a Canadian indigenous street gang, part of the broader phenomenon of Indigenous-based organized crime (IBOC). It is primarily active in the prairie provinces of Canada, where high concentrations of indigenous people reside. The gang is involved in various criminal activities and has been noted as one of the largest groups in federal prisons.

Simon City Royals.description: The Simon City Royals are a street and prison gang founded in Chicago in 1952 as Simon City, a greaser gang, and later merged with the Ashland Royals in 1969 to form the Simon City Royals. They are heavily involved in drug trafficking, racketeering, and loan sharking, and have expanded to other states, notably Mississippi. The gang is predominantly white and is one of the oldest and largest white gangs in the US.

Locale di Piscopio.description: A 'ndrangheta criminal organization based in the Piscopio district of Vibo Valentia, Italy, founded between 2009 and 2010. It is involved in drug trafficking and extortion, operating in the Vibo Valentia area, particularly in Piscopio and Stefanaconi. The group has connections with other 'ndrangheta clans in Calabria and Turin. It was involved in the Stefanaconi feud (2011-2012) and has been targeted by multiple police operations.

Commisso 'ndrina.description: A powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Siderno, Calabria, Italy, with a branch in the Greater Toronto Area, Canada. Founded in the 1930s by Antonio Macrì, it is involved in international drug trafficking, money laundering, and extortion. The clan rose to prominence under Cosimo Commisso and has been engaged in feuds with rival 'ndrine, notably the Costa 'ndrina. It remains active in Italy, Canada, and the United States.

descriptions: Commisso 'ndrina is a clan within the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Siderno is also home to one of the 'Ndrangheta's biggest and most important clans, heavily involved in the global cocaine business and money laundering.

Clan dei Cursoti.description: Italian criminal organization originating from Catania, active in the 1970s-1980s with bases in Milan and Turin. It split into two factions, the Milanese and Catania branches, and was involved in conflict with the Santapaola family. The clan is currently led by the Di Stefano brothers.
